one_liner: CMG -1.1% to $32.97 — RSI 61.9 approaching overbought and DCF shows -126% overvaluation; no technical or fundamental setup here.

price_action.intraday_story: CMG sold off on very low volume — 0.58x average. No particular catalyst driving the down move; just general consumer discretionary relative weakness.

news_catalyst.headline: No Significant CMG News
news_catalyst.summary: No company-specific news. CMG continues to trade at a significant premium to intrinsic value on the strength of its consistent same-store sales growth and operational excellence. No near-term catalyst identified.

technical.summary: RSI 61.9 approaching overbought on the recent run. Above EMA50 ($30.96) — technically in uptrend. But DCF at $14.59 (-126% overvalued) and Grade C fundamentals make this a valuation risk.

wyckoff_narrative: No Wyckoff events. Stock near the upper end of its recent range on low volume. More likely distribution than accumulation at current levels.

valuation.summary: DCF at $14.59 — CMG trades at 2.3x fair value. The brand and operational moat justify some premium but not this much.

key_strengths: Consistent same-store sales growth and operational efficiency
key_concerns: DCF -126% overvalued — significant premium to fair value
Consumer spending slowdown threat to premium casual dining
fundamentals.summary: Grade C — solid operator but overvalued. Not a setup to enter here.

risks: DCF overvaluation by 126% provides no floor on a market correction
Consumer spending slowdown risk for premium fast casual
